summary:
Octogenarians are at significantly elevated risk for complications from carotid endarterectomy due to reduced neurologic reserve and higher baseline morbidities. Guidelines generally avoid recommending CEA in this population because the procedural risks outweigh potential benefits.  

(End of Round)
